zotero.abstractNote: The rapid proliferation of autonomous agents necessitates robust safety evaluation frameworks. While existing benchmarks primarily focus on single-turn or human-agent interactions, agent-to-agent (A2A) communication introduces unique safety challenges, such as the propagation of harmful content and the leakage of sensitive information across multi-turn dialogues. In this paper, we introduce ConVerse, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate contextual safety in A2A conversations. ConVerse features a taxonomy of 12 safety categories, including privacy leakage, social bias, and harmful content generation, specifically tailored to the dynamics of A2A interactions. We evaluate 10 state-of-the-art LLMs across various configurations, revealing that even advanced models struggle to maintain safety in multi-agent settings. Our findings highlight the critical need for context-aware safety mechanisms in agentic systems.

extra.Publication Title: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: EACL 2026
